title: A11y Click: React Fiber + CDP Trusted Click Fallback for Chrome SPAs
desc: Chrome React SPA buttons expose UIA Invoke but fire no trusted event. Fallback chain: LegacyIA -> WM_LBUTTON -> TryCdpTrustedClick (Input.dispatchMouseEvent) -> TryReactFiberClickAsync (__reactProps.onClick direct call). Deployed commit a575bece.
steps: CLI click tier order in A11yActions.Click.cs: (1) LegacyIA.DoDefaultAction() focusless, (2) WM_LBUTTON to element hwnd, (3) TryElectronSurfaceClick() WM_LBUTTON to Chrome_RenderWidgetHostHWND, (4) TryCdpTrustedClick() Input.dispatchMouseEvent isTrusted=true, (5) TryReactFiberClickAsync() __reactProps.onClick JS call.

TryCdpTrustedClick() verifies success via pixel-diff check (>2% change = ok). If click verified, returns immediately. TryReactFiberClickAsync() calls __reactFiber.memoizedProps.onClick for React-controlled buttons where isTrusted enforcement blocks CDP dispatchMouseEvent.

Known gap: Scenario/ActionExecutor path does NOT include CDP or React fiber tiers -- only UIA Invoke + element-rect SendInput. If Invoke silently succeeds on React buttons (S_OK but no DOM event), the action returns prematurely. Full fix pending; CLI path already handles this.

pixel-diff skip for focus-only elements: Text/Edit/Document/Custom ControlType don't change visually on click -- CDP TrustedClick now skips pixel-diff for these types and trusts the dispatchMouseEvent result directly
author: claude
